Returns and rankings Annualised returns Absolute returns Name 3Y 5Y 10Y All Fund returns +21.7% +15.4% +14.6% +14.4% Category average ( Equity Large Cap ) +15.1% +15.0% +13.2% -- Rank ( Equity Large Cap ) 12 12 7 -- Understand terms Expense ratio A fee payable to a mutual fund house for managing your mutual fund investments. It is the total percentage of a company's fund assets used for administrative, management, advertising, and other expenses. Tax A percentage of your capital gains payable to the government upon exiting your mutual fund investments. Taxation is categorized as long-term capital gains (LTCG) and short-term capital gains (STCG) depending on your holding period and the type of fund. Exit load A fee payable to a mutual fund house for exiting a fund (fully or partially) before the completion of a specified period from the date of investment. Stamp duty A form of tax payable for the purchase or sale of an asset or security.

Stamp duty A form of tax payable for the purchase or sale of an asset or security. Exit Load 01 Jan 2019 -- 05 Mar 2014 Exit load of 0.25% if redeemed within 7 days 01 Jan 2013 Exit load of 1% if redeemed within 1 year Exit load, stamp duty and tax Exit load Nil Stamp duty on investment: 0.005% (from July 1st, 2020) from July 1st 2020 Tax implication If you redeem within one year, returns are taxed at 20%. If you redeem after one year, returns exceeding Rs 1.25 lakh in a financial year are taxed at 12.5%.

This scheme was made available to investors on 12 Oct 1993. Ajay Kumar Solanki is the Current Fund Manager of ICICI Prudential Nifty Next 50 Index Direct Growth fund. Latest NAV as of 06 May 2026 is ₹66.59. The ICICI Prudential Nifty Next 50 Index Direct Growth is rated Very High risk. Minimum SIP Investment is set to ₹100. Minimum Lumpsum Investment is ₹100. ; Investment Objective The objective of the fund is to invest in companies whose securities are included in Nifty Next 50 Index and to endeavor to achieve the returns of the above index as closely as possible.
